response:
you need to add specific package references to your windows project file to resolve this missing assembly issue.
ensure that the presentation framework packages are explicitly included within the windows specific configuration section of your solution.
cousin projects often face similar binding issues when mixing modern dot net core frameworks with legacy windows presentation libraries.
installing the microsoft windows desktop libraries package should provide the necessary runtime components for your wpf window to load correctly.
this approach keeps your main application cross platform while satisfying the windows specific dependency requirements without downgrading your core project.
